creator: petefrench@ticketswitch.com
text:
> It's not a bug, we don't install a static library specifically,
> so no other ports will become linked with the static one
> accidentally.  What do you need the static library for?

We have a lot of software written in Objective C, which currently
links aginst the shipped libobjc. The switch to clang in FreeBSD-9
means that this library is going away as part of the base system, so
I have been working on a port of David Chisnalls libobjc2 to FreeBSD
to provide an alternative (that port was submitted on Friday).

libobjc2 has all of the new language features of Objective C 2,
including the threading stuff, which means that it has dependency on
grand central despatch. hence I made the port depend on libdespatch.

But the port of libdespatch doesnt contain static libraries, so linking
my code doesnt work. i.e. I can no longer generate a static executable
of my software which will run on a system without the port installed.
We started generating static executables for speed, but the ability to
run without needing any extra libraries has proved extremely useful
over the years. The main reasin is, admittedly, speed - but that as
actually fairly significant the last time I benchmarked it.

All the other library ports install both the static and the dynamic
version of the library, so I am slightly surprised that this was a deliberate
feature. Is there something about libdespatch which makes accidenatly
linking against a static library risky in a way that linking against
a static png library does not ? I am hazzarding a guess here that what you
are trying to guard against is changes in the kerenl ABI maybe ? But that
should surely be constant across a single release ?

Anyway, thats the long answer - the short answer is "I need a static library
to generate static executables", but I thought that was kind of facetious :-)

If there are good reasons that this port needs to behave differently to
other library ports then I understand, but could it be added as an option
at least ?

thanks,

-pete.
